issues: Bottom-right results band is overcrowded: the '+11.7' delta annotation, the '70.8' label, the LEARNED bar, and the two efficiency badges ('4× fewer params', '~38% fewer FLOPs') all collide in a tight cluster. The '+11.7' bracket visually overlaps the badges and the fixed/LEARNED bars, muddying the single most important number.
Efficiency-badge subtext appears clipped at the right panel border (e.g. '...ms. acc'/'@K=4' running into or past the blue rounded border). Right edge of Panel B has no breathing room.
'+ β_t·π_m' label (arrow a8) sits flush against the top-right panel border and reads as nearly cut off; it also crowds the 'MoR depth router' box and the 'weight-shared ×K' label.

The ×K weight-shared loop is one of the top-3 emphasis elements but is NOT unmistakable: 'weight-shared ×K' is present as text, yet there is no bold curved loop arrow visibly wrapping the 'Shared transformer block'. It currently reads as a caption, not a recursion loop.

Grey control bars (none / random / fixed) lack their value labels (57.5, 57.5, ≈57). Only '70.8' is labeled, so the 'flat baseline then jump' story is carried by bar height alone; the quantitative 'no gain over random' claim isn't anchored on the bars.
'honest negative (no gain over random)' reads as a chart subtitle rather than a tag attached to the FIXED bar, so it isn't clearly pointing at the fixed-biology bar it's meant to indict.
'Shared transformer block f_θ' header text runs close to the 'MoR depth router' box in the top-right; the top row of Panel B feels tight.
revision_instructions: 1) Give the results band more room and de-collide it: shrink the mini bar chart slightly and shift it left; move the two efficiency badges DOWN-and-fully-inside the panel so their subtext ('@K=4', 'matched acc.') clears the right border by a visible margin — never let text touch the rounded panel edge. 2) Redraw the '+11.7' as a clean vertical green bracket/arrow spanning from the top of the random/fixed baseline (~57.5) up to the top of the LEARNED bar (70.8), placed in the empty space directly above the bars, NOT overlapping the badges. 3) Add value labels on all four bars: '57.5', '57.5', '≈57' (grey, above each control bar) and '70.8' (green, bold). 4) Turn the 'weight-shared ×K' into a BOLD curved blue loop arrow that visibly wraps over the top of the 'Shared transformer block f_θ' box and returns to its input, with the '×K' label riding on the loop — this must read as recursion at a glance. 5) Move the '+ β_t·π_m' label inward off the border and route arrow a8 (graph→MoR) as a thin dashed line so it's clearly secondary and not clipped. 6) Re-anchor the 'honest negative (no gain over random)' tag as a small callout with a leader line pointing specifically at the FIXED bar, not as a chart subtitle. 7) Add a small margin between 'Shared transformer block' and 'MoR depth router' so the top row of Panel B doesn't crowd.
